questions.text: How do workers log into email and important accounts?

options.ai_rec: Danger: Add two-step login now. Passwords alone can be stolen.
Risky: Turn on two-step login for ALL accounts.
Good: Add two-step login to banking and admin too.
Perfect! Check this once a year.

questions.text: How are worker accounts set up?

options.ai_rec: Danger: Give each person their own login. Sharing is unsafe.

options.text: Each person has a login, but old ones stay
options.ai_rec: Risky: Remove logins when people leave.

options.text: Each person has a login, reviewed yearly
options.ai_rec: Good: Try to review every 6 months.

options.text: Each person has a login, auto-reviewed
options.ai_rec: Perfect! Keep this running.

questions.text: What happens when a worker quits or is fired?

options.text: Access removed after a week or more
options.ai_rec: Danger: Remove access on their last day.
Risky: Remove access within 24 hours.
Good: Try to automate this.

options.text: Access removed automatically on last day
options.ai_rec: Perfect! Keep this running.

questions.text: Who can add new programs to work computers?

options.ai_rec: Risky: Only let IT staff add programs.
Okay: Have IT staff approve instead.

options.text: Only IT staff can add programs
options.ai_rec: Good: Make a list of approved programs.

options.text: Only IT staff + approved list only
options.ai_rec: Perfect! Keep the list updated.

questions.text: How often do you save copies of important files?

options.ai_rec: Danger: Start automatic backups today. You could lose everything.
Risky: Back up weekly or daily.
Good: Back up daily to lose less data.
Perfect! Test restoring files monthly.

questions.text: Where do you keep backup copies?

options.text: Same place as the original files
options.ai_rec: Danger: Keep one copy in a different place (cloud or off-site).

options.text: External hard drive at the office only
options.ai_rec: Risky: Add cloud backup for safety.
Good: Test restoring from cloud.
Perfect! Keep testing regularly.

questions.text: How do you protect private customer or business data?

options.ai_rec: Risky: Turn on encryption (data locking) on all devices.
Okay: Protect ALL private files.
Good: Lock entire hard drives too.

options.text: All devices have full lock protection
options.ai_rec: Perfect! Keep encryption keys safe.

questions.text: Do you know where all your private data is stored?

options.ai_rec: Risky: Make a simple list of what data you have and where.
Okay: Finish the list for all data.
Good: Mark what data is private vs public.
Perfect! Review this list yearly.

questions.text: How are computers and phones locked?

options.ai_rec: Danger: Add passwords or PINs to all devices now.
Risky: Lock ALL devices.
Good: Add auto-lock and fingerprint/face unlock.
Perfect! Keep this updated.

questions.text: How long before devices lock themselves when not used?

options.ai_rec: Risky: Set auto-lock to 5 minutes or less.
Okay: Change to 5 minutes.
Good: Change to under 5 minutes.
Perfect! Keep this setting.

questions.text: What virus protection is installed?

options.ai_rec: Danger: Install virus protection on all devices now.
Risky: Install on ALL devices.
Good: Turn on automatic updates.
Perfect! Keep definitions updated.

questions.text: How do you handle software updates?

options.ai_rec: Danger: Turn on automatic updates now.
Risky: Turn on automatic updates.
Good: Update within 7 days.
Perfect! Keep this running.

questions.text: How is your Wi-Fi set up?

options.ai_rec: Risky: Create a separate Guest Wi-Fi for visitors.

options.text: Guest network exists but not separated
options.ai_rec: Okay: Turn on network isolation.
Good: Add extra separation for sensitive data.
Perfect! Keep networks separated.

questions.text: Do you use a firewall (network barrier)?

options.ai_rec: Danger: Turn on firewall on router and computers.
Okay: Set up rules to block unwanted traffic.
Good: Add monitoring and logs.
Perfect! Review logs monthly.

questions.text: Did you change the default passwords on routers and network gear?

options.ai_rec: Danger: Change all default passwords now. They are public knowledge.
Risky: Change ALL default passwords.
Good: Write them down and change yearly.

options.text: All changed + written down + changed yearly
options.ai_rec: Perfect! Keep changing them.

questions.text: How do you protect work done from home or outside the office?

options.ai_rec: Danger: Require VPN (secure tunnel) for all remote work.
Risky: Require VPN for ALL remote workers.

options.text: VPN required for all remote work
options.ai_rec: Good: Add two-step login and device checks.
Perfect! Keep this running.

questions.text: Do you have a plan for what to do if you get hacked?

options.ai_rec: Danger: Write a simple checklist: 1. Disconnect 2. Change passwords 3. Call for help.

options.text: We talked about it but nothing written
options.ai_rec: Risky: Write it down and share with everyone.
Good: Test the plan once a year.
Perfect! Keep it updated.

questions.text: When did you last test restoring files from backup?

options.ai_rec: Danger: Test restoring files now. Backups might not work.
Risky: Test every 6 months.
Good: Test every 3 months.
Perfect! Keep testing on schedule.

questions.text: How often do workers learn about spotting scam emails?

options.ai_rec: Danger: Train workers on scam emails now.
Risky: Train at least once a year.
Good: Add fake scam email tests.
Perfect! Keep tests running.

questions.text: Do you know who to call if you think you've been hacked?

options.ai_rec: Risky: Make a contact list with names and numbers.
Okay: Write down a full contact list.
Good: Add who calls who first.
Perfect! Review yearly.
